title: AirTraq Camera
content_markdown:
#### Indications
- Intubation with the Airtraq intubation device.

#### Procedure

- Remove the existing eye piece from the Airtraq.

- Place the camera onto the Airtraq until it is fully inserted. The screen should be in the opposite direction to the Airtraq blade tip (see **Figure 1**).

- The camera will turn on automatically (in 4 seconds).

- If not attached to the Airtraq, the camera can be turned on manually by pressing the power on pin (see **Figure 2**).

- If the camera becomes dislodged during intubation a warning message will show “blade detached”.

- The initial start up screen displays menu and action icons along with the camera image.

- The icons are activated by touching the screen and will work when pressed while wearing gloves.

- Any moisture/occlusion on the Airtraq light bulb will obliterate any view on the Airtraq and the camera.

- When the camera is turned on an icon at the top right of the screen shows the remaining minutes of use and battery charge status. The battery charge status will blink orange if battery falls below 20 minutes of use.

- Pressing the yellow 180 button will rotate the screen 180\xb0 (see **Figure 3**).

- Paediatric mode must be manually selected for paediatric patients (otherwise the camera image will be too bright). In most cases the camera will detect a paediatric blade and the icon will flash. Press the purple paediatric button to activate paediatric mode.

- Please do not use the red record icon.

- The device will automatically shut down if not used after 30 minutes if inserted onto Airtraq or 5 minutes if not attached to Airtraq.

- To manually turn off the camera press the “power off” icon on the main menu.

- To remove the camera lift up and pull the camera away from the Airtraq.

Figure 2

Figure 3
